title: Malvastrum tricuspidatum
taxonomy: Malvastrum tricuspidatum is classified under the family Malvaceae, commonly known as the mallow or hibiscus family. This family comprises over 200 genera and 2,300 species of flowering plants. Malvastrum tricuspidatum is a member of the genus Malvastrum, which is native to tropical Africa. The species is further categorized as a shrub or small tree, characterized by its three-lobed leaves and showy flowers. The classification of Malvastrum tricuspidatum reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Malvaceae family.

intro: Malvastrum tricuspidatum, also known as the three-lobed Malvastrum, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the family Malvaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments including digestive issues and skin conditions.
